outcome_contract.why: Inline styles and arbitrary layout classes make future theme changes, dark-mode maintenance, and design-system upgrades fragile. Centralising all layout through DS primitives and eliminating inline styles ensures that token changes propagate correctly throughout the app.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All reception app production components are free of inline `style={{` overrides; arbitrary Tailwind bracket values are either replaced with tokens or formally suppressed with justification; raw flex/grid layout class patterns are migrated to DS layout primitives.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: 215 raw flex/grid instances; 2 inline style overrides; 3 arbitrary bracket values
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Layout breakage risk if DS primitive defaults differ from current flex defaults; must verify equivalence per component
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — approach must address visual equivalence verification

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: _BookingTooltip uses JS-computed coordinates (mouse position); KeycardDepositMenu uses JS-computed absolute position
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: If position coordinates are removed from inline style, dropdown/tooltip will misplace — must keep computed coordinates via alternative mechanism
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — positions using JS state cannot be replaced with pure CSS classes

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Layout/structural change only — no auth, input, or data exposure involved
No logs, metrics, or audit trail affected by layout class changes
Existing RTL tests check component render; layout swap should not break tests but visual equivalence is unverifiable via RTL
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: CI test pass is necessary but not sufficient for visual correctness
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — must define post-build visual validation strategy

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No schema, API, or type changes
CSS class swaps have negligible runtime perf impact; no hot paths affected
Single deploy; rollback is a git revert
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — deploy as one release or per-screen groups for safety

stage_payload.scope_summary: The reception app's design system compliance audit revealed three classes of non-compliance. Color token usage is already clean (zero hardcoded colors, zero raw Tailwind palette classes). The remaining violations are: (1) inline `style={{` props that bypass the token cascade, (2) arbitrary Tailwind bracket values that hard-code spacing/sizing without tokens, and (3) raw `className="flex ..."` layout patterns that should use DS layout primitives (`<Inline>`, `<Stack>`, `<Cluster>`, `<Grid>`).
goals: Eliminate all inline `style={{` props in production components
Resolve or formally justify all arbitrary Tailwind bracket values
Migrate raw flex/grid layout class patterns to DS layout primitives across all screens
non_goals: Color token audit (already clean — zero violations)
Dark mode token additions or new token definitions
Design changes (component appearance, sizing, spacing values — only structural compliance)
constraints: DS layout primitives (`Inline`, `Stack`, `Cluster`, `Grid`) are available at `@acme/design-system/primitives`
assumptions: No runtime behavior depends on inline position styles being applied as JS (CSS class equivalents exist)
The two `max-h-[...]` values in inbox are functionally necessary viewport-relative heights; DS does not yet have a token equivalent — these may need a new token or a defined justified suppression
`ml-[100px]` in Tooltip is a design decision (100px left offset for room label alignment) that needs a DS spacing token or justified suppression

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: All 3 already suppressed with justification; no action needed
High volume but mechanical; per-screen grouping enables safe parallel execution
[UX/States Moderate]: _BookingTooltip and KeycardDepositMenu must retain `top`/`left` in inline style; `position` and `zIndex` can migrate to classes
[UI/visual Advisory]: gap/alignment defaults not verified per-component
rehearsal_issues.Resolution Required: No — resolved in execution by spot-checking

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: Existing tests provide structural guard; no new test authoring needed for layout swap
